reasoning: The Atlanta Braves are currently priced at around +750 to +800 in mainstream betting markets to win the 2025 World Series. This implies a probability of roughly 11.1% to 11.8%. Removing the sportsbook vigorish (which often inflates futures markets to ~120-130% total implied probability) puts their true market likelihood between 8.5% and 9.5%. FanGraphs models are slightly more optimistic at ~14.8%, but these typically overvalue the top contenders. In addition, recent minor injuries to players like Sean Murphy (late February) and Jurickson Profar (early March), combined with the delayed returns of stars Ronald Acuña Jr. and Spencer Strider, slightly suppress their upside heading into Opening Day. The Polymarket price was 0.10 on March 6. Considering the recent minor injuries and vig-free odds calculations, an estimate of 9% accurately models their odds.
